children.heading: Director; status; appointment and term; removal
children.label: a Director; status; appointment and term; removal
children.text: In the administration of the Congressional Award Program, the Board shall be assisted by a Director, who shall be the principal executive of the program and who shall supervise the affairs of the Board. The Director shall be appointed by a majority vote of the Board, and shall serve for such term as the Board may determine. The Director may be removed by a majority vote of the Board.
formulate programs to carry out the policies of the Congressional Award Program;
establish such divisions within the Congressional Award Program as may be appropriate; and
employ and provide for the compensation of such personnel as may be necessary to carry out the Congressional Award Program, subject to such policies as the Board shall prescribe under its bylaws.

children.heading: Requirements regarding financial operations; noncompliance with requirements
children.label: c Requirements regarding financial operations; noncompliance with requirements

children.text: The Director shall, in consultation with the Board, ensure that appropriate policies and procedures for fiscal control and accounting are established for the financial operations of the Congressional Award Program, and that such operations are administered by personnel with expertise in accounting and financial management. Such personnel may be retained under contract. In carrying out this paragraph, the Director shall ensure that the liabilities of the Board do not in any fiscal year exceed the assets of the Board.

children.label: A
children.text: The independent public accountant conducting the annual audit of the financial records of the Board pursuant to shall determine for each fiscal year whether the Director has substantially complied with paragraph (1). The findings made by the independent public accountant under the preceding sentence shall be included in the reports submitted under .section 807(a) of this titlesection 807(b) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: If the Director fails to substantially comply with paragraph (1), the Board shall instruct the Director to take such actions as may be necessary to correct such deficiencies, and shall remove and replace the Director if such deficiencies are not promptly corrected.
